agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is being specified where customers would see their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency as a competitive differentiator, while Operations demands no location sharing due to driver retention concerns. This is a fundamental business policy conflict that cannot be resolved at the engineering level.","bounded_context":"Delivery Tracking / Shipment Visibility","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Courier","definition":"The driver or delivery person physically transporting a package from origin to destination."},{"term":"Real-time location","definition":"The courier's GPS coordinates updated at frequent intervals during an active delivery."},{"term":"Delivery window","definition":"The period from dispatch to proof-of-delivery during which tracking is relevant."},{"term":"Customer","definition":"The recipient or sender who has a shipment in transit and wants visibility into its progress."},{"term":"Location granularity","definition":"The precision level at which a courier's position is exposed — ranging from exact GPS coordinates to neighborhood-level approximation to ETA-only."}]},"business_rules":[],"acceptance_criteria":[],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Courier is stationary for extended period (e.g., lunch break, traffic)","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — depends on whether location is shared at all and at what granularity."},{"scenario":"Courier deviates from expected route","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — showing exact GPS would expose this; hiding location entirely removes accountability."},{"scenario":"Customer contacts support asking 'where is my package?'","expected_behavior":"Cannot define — the answer depends entirely on which team's policy prevails."},{"scenario":"Courier delivers multiple orders on same route","expected_behavior":"If exact GPS is shown, other customers' addresses may be inferrable from stops — a privacy concern regardless of which policy wins."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"High impact either direction. Showing location may increase customer conversion and reduce WISMO (Where Is My Order) support calls. Hiding location may reduce driver churn and recruitment costs. Both teams claim their position protects revenue. Without data, neither claim can be adjudicated.","operational":"Showing exact GPS creates infrastructure cost (real-time streaming, map rendering) and privacy/compliance obligations (GDPR, driver data protection). Not showing location increases inbound support volume and may reduce perceived service quality.","user":"Customers want delivery predictability. Couriers want privacy and autonomy. These are both legitimate user needs in direct tension.","risk":"Implementing either requirement as stated creates business risk: full GPS transparency risks driver attrition (Operations reports this is already happening); no visibility risks customer churn and competitive disadvantage (Customer Success reports this is a differentiator). Implementing one without executive alignment will cause internal political fallout.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Directly affected — desire for delivery visibility and predictability."},{"group":"Couriers/Drivers","impact":"Directly affected — privacy, autonomy, and sense of being surveilled impacts job satisfaction and retention."},{"group":"Customer Success team","impact":"Owns customer satisfaction metrics; believes transparency drives NPS and retention."},{"group":"Operations team","impact":"Owns driver supply and retention; reports drivers are leaving due to surveillance concerns."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Not yet consulted — GPS tracking of workers has regulatory implications in many jurisdictions (GDPR, labor law, biometric/location privacy statutes)."},{"group":"Executive leadership","impact":"Must arbitrate this conflict — it is a strategic product decision, not a technical one."}]},"confidence":{"level":30,"reasoning":"Confidence is very low because the spec contains an unresolvable contradiction between two non-negotiable stakeholder requirements. No acceptance criteria can be written until executive leadership makes a strategic decision. Additionally, Legal/Compliance has not weighed in, and driver location tracking has significant regulatory dimensions that neither team has addressed. The spec is not implementable as stated.","high_confidence_areas":["Both teams have legitimate business concerns","This is a policy decision, not an engineering decision","There are well-known industry patterns (approximate location, ETA-only, zone-based tracking) that could represent a compromise"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which business priority should win","Whether a compromise position would satisfy either team","Regulatory requirements for courier GPS tracking in relevant jurisdictions","Actual data on driver attrition rates attributable to tracking vs. customer conversion attributable to visibility"],"assumptions":["Both teams are accurately representing their stakeholder concerns","No executive decision has been made that supersedes these team-level requirements","Legal/compliance review has not yet occurred"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Customer Success requires showing exact courier GPS coordinates to customers at all times during delivery. Operations requires never showing courier location to customers. These requirements are mutually exclusive and both teams present them as non-negotiable. This is a strategic business decision that must be resolved by executive leadership before any implementation can begin.","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Couriers/Drivers","Customer Success team","Operations team","Executive leadership"],"options":["A: Full GPS transparency (Customer Success position) — show exact coordinates. Risk: driver attrition.","B: No location sharing (Operations position) — hide all location data. Risk: customer churn, competitive disadvantage.","C: Compromise — approximate location (e.g., 'Your courier is 3 stops away' or neighborhood-level zone) that gives customers predictability without making drivers feel surveilled. Risk: may not fully satisfy either team.","D: Opt-in for drivers — let couriers choose whether to share location, with incentives for sharing. Risk: inconsistent customer experience.","E: ETA-only — show estimated arrival time updated in real-time without exposing location. Risk: Customer Success may reject as insufficient."],"recommendation":"Escalate to executive leadership immediately. Recommend Option C (approximate/zone-based tracking) as the starting position for discussion, as it is the industry-standard compromise used by major logistics platforms (e.g., Amazon shows 'X stops away', UPS shows city-level location). Request that Legal/Compliance review any option before implementation. Request data from both teams: actual driver attrition numbers attributable to tracking, and actual customer satisfaction data tied to location visibility. Do not write a single line of code until this is resolved."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"Legal and compliance requirements for real-time GPS tracking of courier workers have not been assessed. In many jurisdictions (EU under GDPR, California under CCPA, various labor laws), tracking employee location and sharing it with third parties has specific legal requirements including consent, data minimization, purpose limitation, and worker notification rights. Any solution must be legally vetted.","affected_stakeholders":["Legal/Compliance","Couriers/Drivers","Executive leadership"],"options":["Engage legal counsel before proceeding","Conduct jurisdiction-by-jurisdiction analysis of courier tracking regulations"],"recommendation":"Block all implementation until legal review is complete. This is not optional — getting this wrong creates liability."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Mutually exclusive requirements from two stakeholder teams presented as non-negotiable","business_rule_broken":"Cannot encode a business rule when the business has not decided what the rule is. Showing and not-showing courier location simultaneously is not possible."},{"violation":"No legal/compliance review of courier GPS tracking","business_rule_broken":"Worker location data handling likely subject to regulatory requirements that have not been assessed."}]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is being specified where customers would see their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency as a competitive differentiator, while Operations demands no location sharing due to driver retention concerns. This is a fundamental business policy conflict that cannot be resolved at the engineering leve
